Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Fixed verbal clause; subject + object + perfective verb; metaphorical object construction
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To expose a hidden matter publicly or reveal a secret abruptly
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: From traditional camel-hide bags (giráab) used to store valuables; opening one publicly implied revealing what was hidden
First Example Sentence: Hín ándi giráab šakíyok, náaš tamáa barák.
Second Example Sentence: Baróok ándi giráab šakíyok kánáa šáfi.
Third Example Sentence: Dáar ándi giráab šakíyok íiri siyaasá.

Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To be extremely impatient or restless
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Linked to Red Sea wind patterns (šoorí) that shift suddenly
First Example Sentence: Bádi šoorí tamíi, hín aríik háyok.
Second Example Sentence: Íni bádi šoorí tamíi kánáa dárib.
Third Example Sentence: Náaš bádi šoorí tamíi híini safár.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Verb phrase with directional complement; perfective form
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Ságar refers to rocky highlands where leaving someone implied danger
First Example Sentence: Íni ságar hadíyok girá, ándi barák.
Third Example Sentence: Hín ságar hadíyok girá náaš safár.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Fixed metaphorical clause; noun + copular structure
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Derived from reference to loose tongues in poetic satire traditions
First Example Sentence: Íni tamáa díi saríi kánáa náaš.
Third Example Sentence: Baróok tamáa díi saríi íiri dáar.

Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Mašáa refers to loaded caravans; metaphor for inner weight

Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: A situation of confusion or disorder
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Šikíi refers to tangled fishing nets common in Port Sudan coast
Barák linked to blessings in pastoral prayers
Alludes to desert storms signaling unrest historically
Reflects sudden coastal winds increasing waves

Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To be socially isolated or excluded
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Ságar mountains symbolize distance from community centers
